question: [File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setIsAuthorized recursive delegation] Can an authorized operator A under the precondition that isAuthorized[victim][A]=true call setIsAuthorized(B, true, victim) to authorize B, then B calls setIsAuthorized(C, true, victim) to authorize C, violating the invariant that authorization depth should be bounded, causing scoped impact: unbounded transitive delegation chain enabling any address to eventually gain control of victim's Midnight state? Proof idea: chain N authorizations from victim through A->B->C->...->Z, assert each step succeeds and Z can withdraw victim's credit.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: claimContinuousFee + withdrawable underflow] Can the feeClaimer under the precondition that continuousFeeCredit = C and withdrawable = W where C > W call claimContinuousFee(market, C, receiver), violating the invariant that withdrawable >= amount must hold (uint128 underflow), causing scoped impact: arithmetic underflow in withdrawable creating a wrapped-around large withdrawable value enabling lenders to withdraw far more than deposited? Proof idea: fuzz amount in [withdrawable+1, continuousFeeCredit], assert claimContinuousFee reverts due to underflow in _marketState.withdrawable -= toUint128(amount).

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setMarketSettlementFee + take settlement fee spread] Can the feeSetter under the precondition that a buy offer exists at tick T with offerPrice P call setMarketSettlementFee(id, index, P) to set settlementFee = P, causing sellerPrice = offerPrice - settlementFee = 0, violating the invariant that sellerAssets >= 0 and the spread must be non-negative, causing scoped impact: seller receives zero assets while buyer pays full price, all proceeds going to claimableSettlementFee? Proof idea: setMarketSettlementFee to equal offerPrice, take the offer, assert sellerAssets=0 and claimableSettlementFee increases by full buyerAssets.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setIsAuthorized + EcrecoverAuthorizer delegation chain] Can an attacker under the precondition that victim has authorized EcrecoverAuthorizer (isAuthorized[victim][ecrecoverAuthorizer]=true) and attacker has a valid signature from victim authorizing attacker call EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized to authorize attacker, then attacker calls setIsAuthorized(ecrecoverAuthorizer, false, victim) to revoke EcrecoverAuthorizer, then setIsAuthorized(attacker, true, victim) to make authorization permanent, violating the invariant that the victim can always revoke delegated access, causing scoped impact: attacker permanently locks victim's authorization state? Proof idea: execute the three-step sequence, assert victim can no longer use EcrecoverAuthorizer to revoke attacker.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setMarketTickSpacing + setMarketSettlementFee interaction] Can the tickSpacingSetter under the precondition that feeSetter has set high settlement fees for a market call setMarketTickSpacing(id, 1) to enable all ticks, then takers can access very low-price ticks where offerPrice < settlementFee, violating the invariant that take should revert when sellerPrice underflows (offerPrice - settlementFee < 0), causing scoped impact: arithmetic underflow in sellerPrice computation when tick spacing reduction enables sub-fee-price ticks? Proof idea: setMarketTickSpacing(id, 1), create offer at tick where tickToPrice(tick) < settlementFee, attempt take, assert it reverts due to underflow.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setIsAuthorized + take ratifier bypass] Can an authorized operator under the precondition that isAuthorized[maker][operator]=true call setIsAuthorized(maliciousRatifier, true, maker) to authorize a malicious ratifier, then create an offer with ratifier=maliciousRatifier that always returns CALLBACK_SUCCESS, violating the invariant that the maker must explicitly authorize the ratifier used in their offers, causing scoped impact: operator hijacks maker's offer authorization to enable fills under attacker-controlled ratifier logic? Proof idea: operator calls setIsAuthorized(maliciousRatifier, true, maker), create offer with ratifier=maliciousRatifier, take the offer, assert isAuthorized[maker][maliciousRatifier]=true enables the take.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setDefaultContinuousFee + touchMarket + setMarketContinuousFee ordering] Can the feeSetter under the precondition that defaultContinuousFee[loanToken]=0 call setDefaultContinuousFee(loanToken, MAX_CONTINUOUS_FEE) after a market is already created, then call setMarketContinuousFee(id, MAX_CONTINUOUS_FEE) to update the existing market, violating the invariant that setDefaultContinuousFee does not retroactively update existing markets, causing scoped impact: feeSetter must make two separate calls to update both default and existing market fees, creating a window where new markets inherit max fee but existing market has zero fee? Proof idea: create market, setDefaultContinuousFee(MAX), assert market.continuousFee still 0, then setMarketContinuousFee(id, MAX), assert market.continuousFee updated.
